questions.text: Which animal is known as the largest land animal in the world?
questions.explanation: The African Elephant is the largest and heaviest land animal currently living on Earth.

questions.text: Which planet in our solar system is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars is called the Red Planet because its surface is covered in iron oxide, or rust.

questions.text: What part of a plant grows underground and takes in water?
questions.explanation: Roots hold the plant in the ground and absorb water and nutrients from the soil.

questions.text: How many days are there in a normal year?
questions.explanation: A standard calendar year has 365 days, while a leap year has 366.

questions.text: Which organ in the human body is responsible for pumping blood?
questions.explanation: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood throughout the entire body.

questions.text: What is the name of the imaginary line that divides the Earth into North and South?
questions.explanation: The Equator is the middle line that sits at zero degrees latitude.

questions.text: Which gas do humans need to breathe in to stay alive?
questions.explanation: Humans and many other animals need oxygen from the air to breathe and produce energy.

questions.text: In which sport would you use a racket to hit a fuzzy yellow ball?
questions.explanation: Tennis is played on a court where players use rackets to hit a ball over a net.

questions.text: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: The seven continents are Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.

questions.text: What color do you get if you mix red and blue paint?
questions.explanation: Purple is a secondary color made by combining the primary colors red and blue.

questions.text: What do we call a shape that has three sides?
questions.explanation: A triangle is a polygon with three edges and three vertices.

questions.text: Which of these is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions.

questions.text: What kind of bird is famous for being unable to fly but is a great swimmer?
questions.explanation: Penguins have wings that act like flippers for swimming, but they cannot fly in the air.

questions.text: Which force pulls objects down toward the center of the Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the force that keeps us on the ground and causes things to fall when dropped.

questions.text: What is the boiling point of water in degrees Celsius?
questions.explanation: At standard air pressure, water turns into steam when it reaches 100 degrees Celsius.

questions.text: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars appears red because its surface is covered in iron oxide, commonly known as rust.

questions.text: Which of these animals is the largest mammal in the world?
questions.explanation: The Blue Whale is the largest mammal and lives in every ocean on Earth.

questions.text: How many bones does an adult human body have?
questions.explanation: An adult human has 206 bones, though babies are born with about 300 bones that fuse together over time.

questions.text: Which continent is the South Pole located on?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the southernmost continent and is almost entirely covered by a thick layer of ice.

questions.text: What do you call water when it is in its solid state?
questions.explanation: When water is cooled to its freezing point, it turns into a solid called ice.

questions.text: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.explanation: In geometry, a hexagon is a flat shape that has six straight sides and six angles.

questions.text: What part of a plant grows underground and absorbs water from the soil?
questions.explanation: The roots of a plant keep it anchored in the ground and take in the water it needs to grow.

questions.text: Dried grapes are commonly known by what name?
questions.explanation: Raisins are grapes that have been dried out by the sun or a drying machine.

questions.text: In which sport would you use a racket and a feathered shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: Badminton is played by hitting a shuttlecock back and forth over a net using rackets.

questions.text: Which inventor is credited with creating the first practical telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell received the first US patent for the telephone.

questions.text: What material is used to make the center of a modern pencil?
questions.explanation: Pencil 'lead' is actually a mix of a mineral called graphite and clay.

questions.text: Which is the largest and deepest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers more than one-third of the Earth's surface.

questions.text: Which of these materials is a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Copper is a metal that allows electricity to flow through it easily, which is why it is used in wires.

questions.text: How many legs does a spider have?
questions.explanation: Spiders are arachnids, and all arachnids have eight legs, while insects have six.

questions.text: Which family of musical instruments does the violin belong to?
questions.explanation: The violin is part of the string family because it makes sound when its strings are bowed or plucked.

questions.text: Which bird is known for its ability to mimic human speech?
questions.explanation: Parrots are well-known for their ability to imitate various sounds, including human words.

questions.text: What state of matter is an ice cube?
questions.explanation: Ice is the solid form of water that occurs when temperatures drop below freezing.

questions.text: Which continent is covered mostly by ice and has no permanent residents?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is the southernmost continent and is almost entirely covered by a thick ice sheet.

questions.text: Which planet is located closest to the Sun in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Mercury has the smallest orbit and is the closest planet to the Sun.

questions.text: Which part of the human body is responsible for pumping blood to all other parts?
questions.explanation: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the blood vessels of the circulatory system.

questions.text: What is the main ingredient used to make traditional bread?
questions.explanation: Flour, usually made from ground wheat, provides the structure and bulk for bread dough.

questions.text: What sweet liquid do bees collect from flowers to make honey?
questions.explanation: Bees collect sugary nectar from flowers and transform it into honey back at their hive.

questions.text: In which sport do players hit a shuttlecock over a high net using a racket?
questions.explanation: Badminton is played with rackets and a shuttlecock, which must be hit over the net without touching the ground.

questions.text: Which instrument is used to measure the temperature of the air?
questions.explanation: A thermometer is a specialized device that measures how hot or cold something is.

questions.text: Who is famously credited with the invention of the long-lasting electric lightbulb?
questions.explanation: Thomas Edison developed the first practical and commercially successful incandescent lightbulb.

questions.text: What color is created by mixing the primary colors blue and yellow?
questions.explanation: When you mix blue and yellow paint or pigments, the resulting color is green.

questions.text: Which musical instrument has 88 keys and is played by pressing them with your fingers?
questions.explanation: A standard modern piano has a total of 88 keys, including 52 white keys and 36 black keys.

questions.text: What is the name of the process where a caterpillar changes into a butterfly?
questions.explanation: Metamorphosis is the biological process of transformation from an immature form to an adult form.

questions.text: What is the term for a word that has the opposite meaning of another word?
questions.explanation: An antonym is a word that means the opposite of another word, such as hot and cold.

questions.text: Which animal is the largest mammal in the world?
questions.explanation: The blue whale is the largest animal to ever live, even larger than the biggest dinosaurs.

questions.text: How many lungs does a healthy human body typically have?
questions.explanation: Humans have a left lung and a right lung that help them breathe oxygen from the air.

questions.text: On which continent is the Sahara Desert located?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world and covers much of North Africa.

questions.text: What part of a plant grows underground and drinks water from the soil?
questions.explanation: Roots hold the plant in the ground and soak up water and nutrients for the plant to grow.

questions.text: Which of these foods is considered a dairy product?
questions.explanation: Dairy products are made from the milk of mammals, like cows or goats.

questions.text: In which sport do players try to kick a ball into the opposing team's goal?
questions.explanation: Soccer is played mainly with the feet, and the goal is to get the ball into the net.
